title: Windows GUI, Administrative Tools, Settings, and State Inspection · Fault-Isolation Clinic
summary: A realistic windows gui, administrative tools, settings, and state inspection ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in Windows state inspection and least-change plan and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Locate and explain current Settings, Control Panel, search, taskbar, notification, accessibility, power, display, application, account, and update surfaces.
Select Task Manager, Computer Management, Event Viewer, Device Manager, Disk Management, Services, System Configuration, Resource Monitor, Registry Editor, and policy tools by evidence need.
Distinguish application, process, service, scheduled task, startup entry, dependency, account, trigger, resource, and termination state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Windows Navigation
steps.instruction:
debug case — Startup delay, storage pressure, device errors, and update failures send support through many tools without recording effective state or time. The debug variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Locate and explain current Settings, Control Panel, search, taskbar, notification, accessibility, power, display, application, account, and update surfaces.

Inspect the Windows Navigation trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Windows Navigation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for locate and explain current settings, control panel, search, taskbar, notification, accessibility, power, display, application, account, and update surfaces. For CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Windows Navigation, keep Windows Navigation connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Windows Navigation, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

Run the smallest deterministic CompTIA A+ support manifest review and retain the actual observation, rejected inference, next decision, and remaining uncertainty for the debug Event Change Evidence case

Change one CompTIA A+ support user, device, version, hazard, symptom, access need, or competing cause; verify repair, rollback, original and changed cases, prevention, handoff, and controlled equipment transfer limits for the debug Event Change Evidence case

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Event Change Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Event Change Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Startup delay, storage pressure, device errors, and update failures send support through many tools without recording effective state or time. The debug variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Correlate clocks, event channels, update history, reliability, recent changes, reproduction, user context, and changed cases while separating warnings from cause.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Event Change Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Event Change Evidence, keep Event Change Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Correlate clocks, event channels, update history, reliability, recent changes, reproduction, user context, and changed cases while separating warnings from cause.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Event Change Evidence, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Event Change Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Event Change Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Startup delay, storage pressure, device errors, and update failures send support through many tools without recording effective state or time. The debug variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Correlate clocks, event channels, update history, reliability, recent changes, reproduction, user context, and changed cases while separating warnings from cause.

For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Event Change Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: The most recent red event is the root cause of the user’s symptom.
